confirmed.note: draw_boop_face_preview lambda is draw_face_preview (main.cpp:1048-1116) copied near-verbatim: identical ~60-line texture-upload/decode body (glGenTextures/glTexImage2D/AddImage + decode-failed fallback), only id swaps face_preview->boop_face_preview, kFaceSlots->kBoopFaceSlots, expr_label->lbl, s.expression->s.file_stem

false_positives.what_happened: 8 sibling effect classes (Sparkle/Snow/Embers/Confetti/Rings/Rain/Fireflies/Clouds) derive from BaseEffect and each have update(double dt)/render() with the SAME scaffolding: a 'particles_.erase(remove_if...)' decay loop and a 'while((int)particles_.size() < count(N)) { pick_life/pick_color/emit_pos; particles_.push_back(p); }' spawn loop, plus a near-identical 'cv::Mat c = blank(); for(p) draw_particle(c,p,alpha); return c;' render. The shared lines are framework boilerplate of a polymorphic effect family; the actual per-effect physics inside the loops genuinely differs.
false_positives.fix_proposal: For within-file hits where both fragments are method bodies of DIFFERENT classes sharing a common base (detect via 'class X : public Base'), and the matched lines are dominated by a recognized container-spawn/decay idiom (remove_if + while-size<N + push_back), require the DIVERGENT (non-idiom) line ratio to exceed a threshold before reporting. I.e. subtract a per-family idiom skeleton: if >60% of matched tokens are the shared loop scaffolding, suppress.

false_positives.what_happened: BtMonitor/PingMonitor/WifiMonitor/WeatherMonitor each define a thread-lifecycle pair: start(AppState*) { state_=...; running_=true; thread_=std::thread(&X::thread_fn,this); } and stop() { running_=false; if(thread_.joinable()) thread_.join(); }. The stop() bodies are byte-identical and start() differs only by class name and a couple of extra assignments. A cross-file token detector flags these 6-12 line skeletons even though each monitor's scan_devices/thread_fn payload is distinct.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Add a min-distinctive-body filter for cross-file hits: ignore matches whose body is a std::thread start/stop lifecycle skeleton (thread_=std::thread(&...,this); running_=...; joinable()/join()). More generally, gate cross-file reports on a minimum count of non-boilerplate statements (>=N unique non-RAII/non-lifecycle stmts), so 6-8 line ctor/start/stop/dtor skeletons that recur across a class family don't fire.
